choice: Railway Postgres backups + manual pg_dump; zero third-party telemetry
rationale: Three binding priors converge on this choice. bfe61279 already commits the persistence layer to Railway Postgres, so Railway's built-in backup mechanism is the natural and already-implied backup story, supplemented by manual pg_dump for portability. db09d3a5 establishes a deliberate containment posture — OAuth tokens encrypted at rest, secrets only in Railway env vars, API access strictly read-only — which is incompatible with routing application telemetry (stack traces, request payloads, slow-query details) through a third-party error-tracking SaaS that could surface financial account data. 3f1b6b21 explicitly rejects any third-party aggregator touching financial data, and an error-tracking SaaS is architecturally equivalent from a data-exposure standpoint. Together these decisions draw a clear boundary: financial data touches only the Railway deployment and E*TRADE, ruling out any third-party observability service in v1.
